description_long: This object is a hand-forged, blacksmith-style knife with a single-edged, straight blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade is seamlessly integrated into a twisted, looped handle, which features decorative bulges and a curled end, typical of traditional Viking or medieval utility knives. The entire piece appears to be made from a single piece of dark, forged metal, giving it a rustic and robust appearance. The handle's open loop design allows for easy hanging or attachment to a belt, and the lack of a guard suggests it is intended for utility rather than combat. The surface is matte with subtle textural variations from the forging process, and the blade edge is visibly sharp. There are no visible engravings or inlays, emphasizing its utilitarian craftsmanship.
description: A hand-forged, single-edged knife with a twisted, looped metal handle and a sharp, straight blade. The entire object is dark metal with a rustic, matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the straight blade and the twisted, looped handle with decorative bulges.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the circular, open loop of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the blade's edge and the continuous metal construction of the handle.
description_view_3: Blade-forward view emphasizing the pointed tip and the ergonomic curve of the handle.
attribute_handle_design: twisted, looped, forged metal with decorative bulges and a curled end
